subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with comprehensive contextual background on Iansaâs market position and regulatory environment.
Research question is clearly stated, tightly focused and sustained as the organising thread throughout the investigation.
Analytical frameworks (Ansoff, BCG, STEEPLE) are introduced coherently and directly linked to the research question.
weaknesses: Methodology description lacks explicit justification of source selection criteria and discussion of data reliability or limitations.
STEEPLE analysis omits several dimensions (e.g. technological, environmental), reducing methodological comprehensiveness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student excels in framing the topic and maintaining a sharply focused research question, fully satisfying the top-band descriptors in those strands. However, the methodology strand falls short of the highest band due to limited justification of source choices and incomplete coverage of the STEEPLE framework. On balance, this warrants a high-end mark of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Source material is applied coherently to support analysis points, and specialist Business Management terminology is used accurately and consistently.
Frameworks and industry concepts (e.g. portfolio analysis, market development) are explained clearly, demonstrating coherent subject knowledge.
weaknesses: Several key data points lack precise in-text citations, undermining the rigor of source application.
Discussion of survey and statistical data omits commentary on methodology or sampling bias, weakening evaluation of data reliability.
Explanations of some industry specifics remain surface-level, limiting the depth of subject knowledge application.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates mostly relevant and appropriate application of sources and clear subject knowledge, but inconsistent citation practice and occasional reliance on unsubstantiated statements prevent top-band performance. A mid-band score of 4 out of 6 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Research draws on a solid mix of primary and secondary sources that are clearly linked to the research question, demonstrating good research quality.
Analytical tools are applied logically, yielding clear, well-supported insights into Iansaâs strategic adaptations.
Arguments are well developed, with conclusions and recommendations flowing coherently from the presented evidence.
weaknesses: Analysis remains predominantly qualitative with limited quantitative interrogation of data or competitor benchmarks.
Evaluation lacks systematic consideration of alternative strategies, risk assessments or model limitations.
Counter-arguments and deeper critical evaluation (e.g. competitor responses, financial modelling) are only briefly addressed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows good research quality and clear analysis with partially critical evaluation, aligned with the upper reaches of the 4â6 band for each strand. While the work does not exhibit the exceptionally sophisticated depth or evaluation required for higher marks, a solid performance across all strands justifies a mark of 6 out of 12.

strengths: Report structure fully meets IB requirements: title, word count, contents page, clear section headings, appendices and bibliography are all present.
Section sequencing and signposting facilitate easy navigation through the analytical narrative.
weaknesses: Layout and formatting are uneven: inconsistent fonts and sizes, misaligned tables, and low-resolution images detract from professional presentation.
Citation and footnote formatting is inconsistent, interrupting reading flow in places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is logically structured and complete, satisfying all organisational requirements, but visual and formatting inconsistencies place it in the mid-band. A score of 3 out of 4 appropriately reflects these strengths and minor presentation flaws.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research questionââÂ¿CÃ³mo Iansa adecuÃ³ su lÃ­nea de endulzantes a las nuevas condiciones del mercadoâ¦?ââis explicitly stated, tightly worded and remains the organising thread for the entire investigation.  
â¢  Each analytical section (STEEPLE, Ansoff, BCG) is framed as evidence to address the question, and the conclusion/projections return directly to it.  
â¢  No significant drift or introduction of subsidiary questions is observed; focus is maintained from introduction through conclusions.  
Hence the strand meets the top-band requirement of a **clearly stated, well-focused question that is effectively connected to discussion**; a score of 6 is justified.

The conclusion draws directly on earlier findings, summarising impacts of regulation and consumer trends and linking them to Iansaâs portfolio changes.  
â¢  Arguments are generally well developed and recommendations (e.g., phasing out âlightâ sugar, expanding stevia range) stem from the presented evidence, showing partial critical evaluation.  
â¢  Nonetheless, alternative strategic options, risk assessments, and limitations are touched on only briefly, so the discussion does not achieve the nuance expected for the top band.  
Thus the strand fits the **well-developed argument with partially critical evaluation** descriptor (4-6 band); a score of 6 is appropriate.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of Business Management conceptsâSTEEPLE, Ansoff, BCGâand applies them coherently to Iansaâs context.  
â¢  Descriptions of each framework are accurate and allow the reader to follow subsequent applications.  
â¢  Depth is uneven: explanations of sugar-industry specifics (e.g., production economics, consumer-behaviour data) remain surface-level, and quantitative insights stop at descriptive statistics.  
Thus, knowledge is **clear and coherent but not fully exploited**, aligning with the 3-4 band; a score of 4 is appropriate.

The student employs specialist Business Management language (p.ej., âpenetraciÃ³n de mercadoâ, âcuota de mercadoâ, âproducto interroganteâ) consistently and accurately in Spanish, and differentiates clearly between endulzante categories.  
â¢  Concepts such as diversification, market development and portfolio analysis are applied correctly throughout.  
â¢  Minor spelling slips (âsucralosa/surcalosaâ, âSTEPPEâ vs âSTEEPLEâ) are present but do not impede clarity or betray misunderstanding.  
Consequently, the work meets the descriptor for **accurate and consistent use of terminology and concepts**, warranting the top-band score of 6.

Analytical tools (Ansoff, BCG, STEEPLE) are applied logically and lead to reasoned insights about Iansaâs strategic adjustments.  
â¢  Interpretations connect tool outcomes to market behaviour (e.g., classifying stevia products as âestrellasâ and evaluating their revenue potential).  
â¢  However, the analysis remains primarily qualitative; it rarely interrogates assumptions, omits counter-arguments, and lacks robust financial or market-share modelling.  
The result is **clear analysis with supporting evidence** but without sophisticated depth, meeting the upper end of the 4-6 band; a score of 6 is given.

Most tables, charts and quotations are meaningfully integrated to support points about shifting demand and portfolio changes; the annual-report graphics and survey statistics feed directly into the analyses.  
â¢  Nonetheless, several figures in the narrative lack in-text citation, and some data (e.g., market-share percentages post-2014) are asserted without reference.  
â¢  Occasional reliance on general statements (e.g., âlos consumidores prefierenâ¦â) weakens precision.  
Given the **mostly relevant and appropriate** but not consistently rigorous application of sources, the strand fits the 3-4 band; a score of 4 is merited.

The student draws on a reasonable mix of annual reports, academic texts and national press, and selects two strategic frameworks (Ansoff, BCG) plus a STEEPLE analysis appropriate to the topic.  
â¢  Source choice shows some awareness of relevance (e.g., Iansa memorias anuales for primary data; local media for consumer-trend evidence).  
â¢  However, the methodology section is brief and largely descriptive; there is limited justification of source selection criteria, no explanation of data reliability checks, and no discussion of limitations or alternative methods.  
These features correspond to the **3-4 band** (âgenerally relevant sources/methods with some evidence of informed selectionâ). A score of 4 is awarded.

The investigation is grounded in a solid set of primary (company reports) and secondary (news, academic, governmental) sources that are explicitly linked to the question.  
â¢  Data on sales volumes, market shares and legislative impacts are woven into the analytical sections, demonstrating **clear relevance**.  
â¢  While breadth is commendable, depth of quantitative data (e.g., post-2019 figures, competitor benchmarks) is limited, preventing the research from reaching an âexcellentâ standard.  
Overall, the evidence supports a **good level of research quality**, consistent with the 7-9 band; a mid-band score of 8 is awarded.

The layout generally follows academic conventions (consistent margins, bibliography, figures labelled), and basic formatting requirements (word count, title, page numbers) are met.  
â¢  However, visual consistency is uneven: varying font styles/sizes, occasional mis-aligned tables, and low-resolution images detract from professional appearance. Citation formatting is inconsistent, and some footnotes interrupt reading flow.  
â¢  These issues place the work in the **1-2 band**, where some layout considerations are missing or incorrect; a score of 2 is assigned.

The student states a precise topicâthe adaptation of Iansaâs sweetener line to declining national sugar consumptionâand immediately situates it within the Chilean sugar industry, offering clear background on Iansaâs scale, recent market shifts and the regulatory context.  
â¢  The introduction explains *why* the issue matters (healthâdriven demand changes, factory closures, market leadership at stake) and *what* will be examined (product-portfolio shifts, strategic tools).  
â¢  Key terms (e.g., âlÃ­nea de endulzantesâ, âcambio de mercadoâ, âLey de Etiquetadoâ) are defined or exemplified, giving readers an assured, comprehensive understanding of scope and significance.  
Overall, the topic is communicated **accurately and effectively** with ample contextual explanation, fully satisfying the 5-6 band descriptors; a score of 6 is awarded.

All required structural elementsâtitle page with word count, contents page, clear section headings, appendices, bibliographyâare present and logically ordered, enabling easy navigation.  
â¢  Analytical tools and supporting documents are placed in appendices and signposted within the text, fulfilling IB presentation expectations.  
â¢  No significant structural omissions are evident.  
Consequently, the report meets the **clearly appropriate structure meeting all requirements** descriptor (3-4 band); the top score of 4 within this band is awarded.

annotations.comment: The methodology description is concise but lacks justification for source selection, discussion of data reliability or acknowledgement of limitations; expand to justify methodological choices.
The research question is explicitly stated, focused and tightly integrated with the investigationâs objectives, serving as a consistent organising thread throughout the report.
Inclusion of a detailed contents page ensures clear navigation and reflects a well-structured presentation scheme.
The analysis section introduction clearly outlines the chosen frameworks (Ansoff and BCG), demonstrating focused method planning directly tied to the research question.
The Ansoff matrix description is accurate but overlong; introducing Iansa-specific data earlier would engage readers and enhance the analytical narrative.

The title clearly communicates the investigationâs scope and significance by defining Iansaâs adaptation focus and providing contextual background, establishing a precise and effective topic framework.
The word count is provided at the start of the document, meeting the IB requirement and demonstrating awareness of the reportâs length constraints.
The background statistics from 2015 and 2019 are presented without inline citations; include precise references for each data point to strengthen application of source material.
This opening sentence is overly long and complex; break it into shorter statements to improve readability and maintain a professional academic tone.
Projection section shows strategic foresight but overlooks potential competitor responses; incorporating scenario risk analysis would strengthen evaluation.
The reliance on a single annual report statistic without comparison to competitor data limits the depth of research quality; incorporate industry benchmarks for contrast.
Effective use of external study data quantifies the lawâs impact (e.g., 25% decline in beverages), directly linking regulatory change to measurable market outcomes.
While legislation details are thorough, the analysis misses explanation of how Iansa specifically responded to these regulations; detail corporate adjustments.
The brand-switching statistic is valuable but lacks discussion of survey methodology or sampling bias; address data reliability for balanced source application.
The Social factor section blends economic and social discussion under one heading; consider reorganising to improve structural clarity in contextual analysis.
The table of contents comprehensively lists all main sections and appendices, facilitating reader orientation and reflecting careful organisational planning.
The figure â361 miles de toneladasâ is footnoted but lacks analysis of its trend impact; integrate commentary on what this decline means for Iansaâs strategic positioning.
The STEEPLE analysis header is included but only political and social factors are examined; expand to cover all STEEPLE dimensions for a comprehensive contextual method.
Development quadrant discussion asserts consumer preference for stevia but omits a citation; reference the specific survey or study to substantiate the claim.
Penetration quadrant is logically identified but lacks quantitative support (e.g., exact market-share figures); include data to justify the placement.
Diversification quadrant explains strategy well but should include a risk assessment of entering new markets to demonstrate critical evaluation.
The âproducto estrellaâ classification is well supported by consumer preference data, illustrating coherent alignment between framework and empirical findings.
The âproducto perroâ analysis correctly identifies declining appeal but would benefit from concrete sales figures to quantify its market deterioration.
The conclusion restates context effectively but could distil key strategic insights and recommendations more succinctly to elevate critical evaluation.
Ansoff-based conclusions highlight growth paths yet omit discussion of model limitations; integrate constraints to demonstrate nuanced understanding.
The description of CERO Kâs 17% market share is informative but would be more impactful if compared directly with sugar line performance for context.
